items.title: Warframe Developers Confirm Console Versions of Soulframe
items.summary: Digital Extremes has officially confirmed in an updated FAQ that the fantasy RPG Soulframe will also be released on consoles, with more details expected at TennoCon 2026.

items.title: Warframe of Mind: Duviri is a great idea that I hate
items.content:
In this week’s Warframe of Mind , I’m taking a break from my time hammering through the many, many frames of Warframe because there are, in fact, Warframes that I have not played. But not many. One of those frames, however, was Kullervo. Farming Kullervo meant, by necessity, going into Duviri and really spending my time in Duviri. You cannot get Kullervo by just occasionally brushing past Duviri; you must farm Duviri. You have to run many cycles with Kullervo specifically, and you must also farm a large number of Duviri-specific resources, and all of this has made me come to a realization:

I really, really don’t like Duviri in practice – which is weird because on paper, I love Duviri.

Duviri is another go at the whole basic idea of having a whole open-world zone, but instead of having the main problem that the existing zones have, it’s much more structured. You have objectives to hit, and you have various side objectives you can pick up. Just as in the other open-world maps, there are unique resources, but most of them are gathered via going around and grabbing them, and only a couple come from the fishing equivalent. There’s no mining and no refining mined gem or minerals. Plus, it all comes into a weird fantasy aesthetic that feels simultaneously timeless and alien.

I do think that there is, in fact, a fixed answer. And the answer is both simple and disappointing: The Drifter’s combat style doesn’t work.

The idea with Duviri was pretty imaginative out of the gate. Instead of most of the other parts of the game, where you get to roll in with a very specific build, Duviri is a mode where you primarily control the Drifter. You have a melee weapon as your primary arsenal and a sidearm as your backup, serving for ranged harassment and specific parry akin to how it works in games like Bloodborne . In theory, all of this is actually good.

In fact, on top of all of these systems some extra variations were added on to melee combat to make things more interesting. And I love the idea of it. The Drifter is not the Operator but is the Operator, and you get to see the ways in which the fundamental essence of the Tenno transcend the Warframes themselves.

But the problems start almost immediately because Drifter combat sucks .

Making melee combat that feels good in an action setting can be tough. Warframe’s melee combat is, honestly, very simple. You mostly hold down one button, and in some contexts, you press a different button. That’s all right because the game’s combat mechanisms are fundamentally about momentum. The simplicity is part of the point. It just really grates on you when all of a sudden you go from a game about hypervelocity ninja with guns to a slow melee game that highlights all of its weaknesses.

Worse yet, Drifter combat exists only here . You have a small suite of abilities, but they aren’t really combat abilities in the same way that most frames have them. You can’t customize your loadout as you can in most of the game. You’re stuck in a slow, less-fun melee build for everything you do in Duviri, and on a fundamental level, that’s the problem. Drifter combat is the centerpiece, and it isn’t fun.

The supposed workaround for this are decrees, of course, but they don’t really work. For one thing, they’re much more relevant for your Warframe than for the Drifter. For another thing, many of the decrees would be useful if you had choices you could build around initially, but you don’t, so you can’t. You have a handful of weapons you can choose between, that’s great, but you can’t build your sidearm to make full use of landing shots. Getting decrees that make your sidearm more useful are nice , but they’re both not a full build and (unfortunately) not consistent.

Compounding all of that, the climax completely abandons all of this. You fight the orowyrm in a way that is otherwise not present in the rest of Duviri; then you just have a Warframe boss fight to cap things off. It makes you wonder why you had to suffer through all the mediocre melee combat just to get here.

The worst part is that it didn’t need to be this way. I don’t mean “we could have just had our normal frames” or something; I mean that you could have actual builds and customization for weapons and gameplay beyond just choosing which melee weapon you want. You know, the way the rest of the game works!

Consider this: What if the Drifter had a specific ability that changed based on the Warframe you select? You don’t even need to do extra work for this; you could literally make it the ability that you get from subsuming the frame on the Helminth. That’s not adding extra work, but it’s actually doing two things at once. One, it’s giving your Drifter more interesting abilities to work with; two, it’s adding an extra consideration to your selection. Do you pick a frame you like that has a worse ability for using alone, like Grendel? Or do you pick a better ability and accept a frame you haven’t fully built as well?

I also feel like there should be more ability to install mods and/or change your sidearm while playing. Not to the extent of the full game, obviously, but there should be something . It could even be tied to how many different weapons you’ve unlocked with Pathos Clamps, give players extra motivation there.

items.title: Warframe – Jade Shadows: Constellations Critique
items.summary: Jade Shadows: Constellations is a very interesting update with some big problems in Warframe. Here is our critique of the update!
items.content:
Alright, it’s been long enough. It’s finally time to fully delve into the latest content update in Warframe and talk about it in detail. Because let’s face it, I don’t think anyone was expecting Jade Shadows to get a sequel quest. But I don’t necessarily think anyone was expecting where this quest would go, and how the rest of the update would land.

Jade Shadows: Constellations landed in a similar way to The Duviri Paradox , where it had a lot of expectations to live up to, but decided to go the subversion route, for better and worse. All the while having some pretty fun post-quest content to enjoy when the game was functioning properly.

Jade Shadows: Constellations focuses on the story of the original quest with the Stalker and his son. But things take a turn where Sirius and Orion, fully grown, appear from different timelines to converge on the current one. They are fighting, and the Stalker has to intervene, find out why they are fighting, and bring an end to the duel. It’s a different direction than the very emotionally driven story of Jade Shadows, and while it started well, it didn’t end well.

The problem the story runs into is the promise of a proper reconciliation between the Stalker and these 2 grown up sons of his. However, they only led up to that promise and never properly delivered on it. Instead, they tried to pull a last-minute attempt at mimicking the same emotional tone as the previous quest, and it fell very flat, for me at least. While it was a decent send-off in that regard, it tried to follow up on different plot threads instead of honing in on the main one.

Suffice it to say, it could have been handled much better considering how cherished Jade Shadows was in 2024 as a very surprising story in Warframe. It felt like it punched above its own weight when all it had to do was follow the same emotional tone from the original quest with the setup that they went with in the still amazing reveal trailer .

Aside from the hit-and-miss cinematic quest, there were a few new activities in Warframe that got added in this update. These activities are genuinely fantastic. The linear approach for Railjack content worked perfectly for these activities. They are missions where you have to aid either Ryoku, Sirius’ Mentor, or Vena, Orion’s Mentor, in taking down the other. Ryoku’s had a really cool moment where you ride a freaking Orowyrm and crash into Vena’s ship with it.

With Vena’s mission, you have to get in a ramsled and fly your way to Ryoku’s ship, and his final fight was way more intricate in comparison to Vena’s, where she is just a bullet sponge that tries to bleed you to death.

Both of their ships are visually distinct and have unique rewards for doing a little puzzle. Speaking of rewards, these might be the most generous missions in the game, where farming out Sirius & Orion , their weapons, and other things just doesn’t feel like a chore. These activities and the reward pools are as perfect as missions can get in Warframe. The same can almost be said about the rewards themselves.

The stars of the update, Sirius & Orion, were really great new additions to the roster in Warframe. Going with a 2-in-1 package that functions a little like Equinox, but with more fluid switching between the characters. I really love the ultimate, its really strong and super stylish.

Pride & Wrath could’ve been the same thing in theory. Where it would still be two weapons but swap with, say, a heavy strike or something. But instead it’s two separate weapons. The passive relying on slam attacks is a little weird, but the buffs from it are worth pulling it off for.

Finally, last and sadly least, is the War Prime . The original weapon came out about a decade ago and is still really strong to this day. But with a Prime version, you’d think there would be a unique passive of some sort…and no beating around the bush here, there wasn’t. It’s very disappointing to see such a cool weapon with awesome stats be just that. I wish they handled this Prime in a similar way to the Venato Prime .

To be as fair as I possibly can, the number of problems this update had was very concerning. It was made more concerning that Digital Extremes had actually warned the player base ahead of time , which I cannot recall the last time that happened. Obviously, these updates are free, but if there are problems that need to be ironed out, a delay should be in order. The main problems I faced were related to the new Railjack content itself.

For starters, the ending mission screens were either showing as failed or not showing the rewards at all. This meant I had to keep going to the new vendor to see what I earned. Sometimes, when playing as Sirius & Orion, I can sometimes teleport to a different part of a map when swapping between them while using abilities. I also saw that Vena would get stuck going to the console in her mission after leaving the Railjack to progress. This isn’t a bug, but Ryoku, for whatever reason, kept pulling my camera to show him activating his stealth. He does this every single time.

But on the up and up, FSR3 is finally available on PC, and it helps the game run even better. They also uplifted the Corpus ship tileset with the new lighting system, and it looks amazing. No complaints here.

items.title: Two Weeks to TennoCon 2026!
items.summary: It’s almost time for Warframe’s annual grand celebration! We’re just two weeks away from TennoCon 2026 on July 10-11. Everything begins on July 10 with the return of our Cosplay Contest and special TennoConcert experience at Canada Life Pla
items.content:
It’s almost time for Warframe’s annual grand celebration! We’re just two weeks away from TennoCon 2026 on July 10-11.

Everything begins on July 10 with the return of our Cosplay Contest and special TennoConcert experience at Canada Life Place, complete with a live orchestra conducted by Warframe composer Matthew Chalmers. Then on July 11, we will be broadcasting live from Canada Life Place again for an entire day of panels, community activities and so much more before everything culminates in TennoLive, where secrets that many thought were lost to time will be laid bare — shaking the Origin System to its very core. Prepare yourself accordingly, Tenno.

Watch for Free Rewards

It wouldn’t be TennoCon without plenty of free Rewards on Twitch and Steam that you can earn just by watching! Be sure to link your Warframe and Twitch accounts ahead of time. Starting on July 10, watch the Cosplay Contest from 7 p.m. ET - 8:30 p.m. ET and TennoConcert from 8:30 p.m. ET to 10 p.m. ET for 30 minutes to earn the following, respectively: - Verminia Cosplay Community Display by Community Creator Luckygoblinnn - Singing Lotus Community Glyph (by Community Creator Cyanfacade) + Built Forma Then on July 11, watch any part of TennoCon from 11 a.m. ET - 4:30 p.m. ET and TennoLive from 4:30 p.m. ET to 6 p.m. ET for 30 minutes each to earn the following: - Haalvu (Primary Weapon) - Mesa Prime Please note: those attending the show in-person with a valid ticket will receive Twitch Drop items via promo codes through an email after TennoCon.

Digital Pack

Further enhance your Arsenal with the TennoCon 2026 Digital Pack, featuring the Hunhullus Ephemera, TennoCon2026 Emote and plenty more! https://store.steampowered.com/app/4447660/Warframe_TennoCon_2026_Digital_Pack/ Whether you’re watching from home or joining us in-person, we can’t wait to experience TennoCon 2026 with you all and reveal a bold new future for Warframe!

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Merch
items.summary: As a sequel to one of Warframe's most beloved updates, we felt Jade Shadows: Constellations deserved a showstopper announcement when we revealed it in April of this year. To that end, we enlisted the legendary team at THE LINE Studio to bri
items.content:
As a sequel to one of Warframe's most beloved updates, we felt Jade Shadows: Constellations deserved a showstopper announcement when we revealed it in April of this year. To that end, we enlisted the legendary team at THE LINE Studio to bring you a first-look at Sirius & Orion with a high-octane animated short worthy of the Stalker's enduring legacy. In honor of that incredible artistic collaboration and the dueling rivals at the center of our latest major update, we're pleased to bring you an exclusive, limited run of Warframe x THE LINE // Jade Shadows: Constellations Merch:

Warframe x THE LINE: Sirius T-Shirt

Featuring a stylized design lifted from the animated short, this boxy, relaxed-fit unisex T-shirt imbues its wearer with the focused precision of Sirius. Heavyweight and made from 100% cotton. Color: Dust.

Warframe x THE LINE: Orion T-Shirt

Featuring a stylized design lifted from the animated short, this boxy, relaxed-fit unisex T-Shirt imbues its wearer with the relentless determination of Orion. Heavyweight and made from 100% cotton. Color: Dust.

Warframe x THE LINE: Celestial Clash Poster

Two unstoppable warriors. Face-to-face (and scythe-to-scythe) clashing high above the wastes of the Orb Vallis. Now immortalized in an awe-inspiring, screenprinted 18" x 24" wall poster.

Warframe x THE LINE Merch Collection

Find harmony in the chaos of Sirius & Orion's eternal battle for the future when you bring home all three items from our latest line of merchandise. Includes both boxy, relaxed fit T-shirts honoring the dueling rivals, as well as their showstopping wall poster.

Special Re-release: Stalker Noggle

The Stalker returns once more in his most adorable physical form! Own the Stalker Noggle and add some charming malevolence to your IRL Base of Operations. Made from polyresin. Box size/Dimensions: 12cm x 12cm x 24cm.

BUY NOW

Please note: Quantities are limited. Get yours while they last!

Nidus Collector’s Statue Available for Pre-Order Now!

The Infestation is spreading beyond the Origin System. The ever-evolving Nidus is getting his very own collectible statue, complete with a commemorative coin and exclusive in-game bonuses. Pre-order yours now from the Warframe Store while quantities last!

Nidus Collector’s Statue

Bring the Infestation’s evolution to your collection with this highly detailed Nidus Statue. Standing at approximately 8.5” (21.59 cm) tall and crafted from polyresin, this collectible captures the grotesque power of Nidus in stunning detail. This purchase also includes an exclusive Nidus Coin crafted from Zinc Alloy at 1.75” (4 cm), an in-game Nidus Noggle and 170 Platinum!

BUY NOW

The Infestation waits for no one — secure your Nidus Collector’s Statue and Coin now!

items.title: Warframe: Afentis Prime Build and Review
items.summary: The Afentis Prime arrived on Wednesday and it stands proudly as a good new addition to the arsenal in Warframe, here is our build and review for it!
items.content:
Back when it was released in 2022 with Veilbreaker , I was not a big fan of the Afentis. Spearguns had undergone a slight rework at that time, sure. But when it came down to brass tacks , the speargun was only really useful for its unique ability when you throw it. Now that it has come out with a prime version with Styanax Prime , it’s gotten a new lease on life. It is basically just “ Afentis , but better,” but it may be very well worth adding to your arsenal in Warframe. Below will be our recommended build for it!

Below will be the build I landed on for the Afentis Prime, which only needs 1 forma for:

This is going to be the standard critical-focused approach. But with the usual damage enhancement mods thrown in, alongside Semi-Rifle Cannonade to keep Critical Delay from knocking down the fire rate, and Radiated Reload to increase the damage output and reload speed, alongside Magnetic Capacity to increase the magazine size and add more damage. Wrap all this up in a little bow with Primary Merciless, and you will have a great Steel Path tested build for it!

Back in 2022, when it launched, the original Afentis was just “ok”. Obviously, the updated stats and mods available to us make the Prime better. But it’s because of that this new version feels so much better to use in endgame content. Its passive ability of throwing it on the ground and offering a unique buff to people standing and shooting in it is honestly more useful during boss fights these days. While I wish there were a way to modify that. It still stands as a really good choice in endgame content. You should absolutely farm (or trade) for it in Warframe !

items.title: Warframe dev says 'no one is celebrating' Destiny 2 winding down: 'A game is healthy when you have competitors'
items.content:
When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Bungie) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 11 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Destiny 2 is slowly closing its doors—its servers aren't shutting down, but Bungie has emphasised that it won't be getting any more content, bar a massive quality of life patch that has left it in a relatively good, but inert and unchanging state.

One might, if they were being cynical and cutthroat, think this is cause for celebration—particularly if they were in charge of the competition, as Warframe is. Not so, says community director and live ops lead Megan Everett in a conversation with Eurogamer .

"Truthfully, it's heartbreaking. It's earth-shattering. I never thought I'd ever see the day where I'd read an article from Bungie about Destiny 2 essentially shutting down.

"I'm speaking personally, but I know people would echo it as well. No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny, and its players and that story. I know that people have obviously compared us in terms of being 'direct competitors' and stuff like that. But I think a game is healthy when you have competitors."

As someone in the MMO space, it's honestly true— World of Warcraft got its act together (at least, when compared to the catastrophe of Shadowlands ) when a mass exodus of fans drifted to Final Fantasy 14 . Conversely, with FF14's stagnation, it's starting to adopt a lot of the measures WoW and other MMOs did with Evercold, shaking things up .

'There is no world where it makes sense,' Warframe creative director says of Destiny 2's demise: 'It's unthinkable' 'Don't blame yourselves' for Destiny 2's end, community lead tells players: 'No words can accurately depict how lucky we are to have shared these worlds with you' Shock, tears, and relief: How Destiny 2's most popular creators reacted to the end of the legendary shooter My point being, healthy competition tends to drive games to be better than one another—and stop me if this is going off the reservation—but I think we can all broadly agree that we like it when videogames are good.

Everett also says it's a shame that Destiny 2's been shown support much too late: "I think what's unfortunate and sad is they announced this was happening, and suddenly people are showing all this support for them. They've been getting a lot of negativity for all this time, and it's like, where was all this love!

Keep up to date with the most important stories and the best deals, as picked by the PC Gamer team.

"Overall, I hate to see that happen to them. I have close friends who play Destiny over Warframe, and ain't mad about it. We have friends over there, to see this happen to them is devastating to the industry. No one saw Destiny going away"

items.title: "Truthfully, it's heartbreaking": Warframe dev on the hole that Destiny 2 leaves behind
items.summary: "A game is healthy when you have competitors."
items.content:
News by Oisin Kuhnke Contributor Published on June 23, 2026 4 comments Follow Destiny 2 There's long been a bit of a rivalry between Destiny and Warframe 's respective communities because of them both being shooty, sci-fi, MMOy joints. But when it comes to the community director behind Warframe, the recent end to new Destiny 2 updates and expansions is no victory, and mostly just finds the whole situation to be "devastating."

Speaking to Eurogamer , community director and live ops lead Megan Everett shared that the Destiny 2 news is "heartbreaking. It's earth-shattering. I never thought I'd ever see the day where I'd read an article from Bungie about Destiny 2 essentially shutting down." Everett noted nobody is "celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny, and its players and that story," and spoke of how even though the two games have been spoken of as in competition, she believes that "a game is healthy when you have competitors, and [Destiny's developers] have done such an amazing job at trying to grow that story regardless of whatever situation they were in."

Everett made specific mention of how one of the saddest elements was how following the end of support announcement, many came out of the woodworks to show support after a long period of negativity. "They've been getting a lot of negativity for all this time, and it's like, where was all this love! People need to put the hate away and show the love, because they could have really used that. I know it was there, but it wasn't as vocal as maybe some of the negativity."

Looping back to those Destiny and Warframe comparisons, Everett also made one thing clear for those looking to switch games: "We tried the PvP thing, and we're not a PvP game! If you're coming to Warframe trying to scratch that itch, you're going to have that itch for a long time." At the very least, Destiny 2's servers will live on, so you'll at least have somewhere to scratch that itch if you need to.

items.title: Warframe: Wrath & Pride Builds and Review
items.summary: Similar to Sirius & Orion, there are two new heavy scythes that came to Warframe in the newest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations. These heavy scythes are basically two separate weapons, but they only have a few differentiating factors. One is critical-based, and the other is status-based. They are perfectly fine...
items.content:
Similar to Sirius & Orion, there are two new heavy scythes that came to Warframe in the newest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations . These heavy scythes are basically two separate weapons, but they only have a few differentiating factors. One is critical-based, and the other is status-based. They are perfectly fine weapons on their own. But they are not the most exciting new melee weapons. Below will be our recommended builds for both!

Below will be the build for Wrath, which you will need no forma for:

Then, below will be the build for Pride, which you will need only 1 forma for:

Wrath will need the usual setup for critical chance and damage. I went for a combination of status mods that give it armor stripping, shield stripping, and radiation for shenanigans mid-combat. I went for Melee Duplicate to ensure the best damage output it can have.

For Pride, I went for a Galvanized Elementalist and Condition Overload build with Melee Vortex to pull in enemies and stack on the status effects. I went for Gas for the area-of-effect damage. Aside from this, these are simple builds to achieve if you have the above mods.

Wrath is a fun weapon to use for crowds due to the critical damage, chopping down anything in its path. Its unique trait is fun to pull off, too, as you can slam down and gain more critical chance depending on how many enemies you hit to get sweet, sweet orange crits. Pride, on the other hand, is definitely more suited for bosses, as Condition Overload allows for more single-target damage. It is not suited for crowds, and its similar trait of slamming down on enemies to get more status chance just isn’t as helpful.

However, both of these weapons are definitely worth grinding for in the new activities in Warframe . They are very easy to acquire, just like Sirius & Orion .

Disclaimer – Digital Extremes provided Wrath & Pride for usage in this article.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Official Soundtrack
items.summary: Fitting for a tectonic brawl between cosmic titans, the Jade Shadows: Constellations Soundtrack features radical rock riffs and catalyzing choral cacophonies. Sirius & Orion may be locked in conflict, but you can bask in harmony with Warfra
items.content:
Fitting for a tectonic brawl between cosmic titans, the Jade Shadows: Constellations Soundtrack features radical rock riffs and catalyzing choral cacophonies. Sirius & Orion may be locked in conflict, but you can bask in harmony with Warframe's latest musical tracks, exquisitely performed and produced in-house by our talented Sound Team.

Where can I listen?

You can find the Jade Shadows: Constellations Official Soundtrack on:
  • Spotify
  • Apple Music
  • YouTube Music
  • Amazon Music
  • Tidal
  • Deezer
  • Bandcamp
Additionally, we've created a new playlist with help from UNFD, the record label who helped us bring ERRA's "Crawl Backwards Out of Heaven" to our unforgettable Jade Shadows: Constellations animated short. Find Warframe x UNFD on Spotify and listen to a specially-curated assortment of tracks from Warframe, ERRA and other artists.

FULL TRACKLIST:

1. Celestial Clash
  • Composer: Matthew Chalmers
  • Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers
  • Choir:Anaïs Kelsey-Verdecchia, Rayna Crandlemire, Kayla Ruiz, Anika Venkatesh, Sarah Mole, Francesca Hauser, Joshua Sutherland, Nathan Gritter, Tyrese Walters, Kai Leung, Martin Gomes, Henry Paterson
  • Choir Orchestration: Francesca Hauser
  • Choir Engineer: Stephen Koszler
2. Sacred Light
  • Composer: Matthew Chalmers
  • Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers
  • Choir: Anaïs Kelsey-Verdecchia, Rayna Crandlemire, Kayla Ruiz, Anika Venkatesh, Sarah Mole, Francesca Hauser, Joshua Sutherland, Nathan Gritter, Tyrese Walters, Kai Leung, Martin Gomes, Henry Paterson
  • Choir Orchestration: Francesca Hauser
  • Choir Engineer: Stephen Koszler
  • Koto: Ray Hickey
  • Shakuhachi: Ron Korb
  • Lyrics: Matthew Chalmers
LYRICS: Where sacred light shines The song of life begins As the shadow of time The protector marches evermore towards silence Glory on high Where the sacred light shines

items.title: Our Refreshed Refer a Friend Program
items.summary: Make note, Tenno — Warframe’s Refer a Friend program update is now live! You can take a look at all the need-to-know details below. Referral Code Changes You can now create and send referral codes from any platform with a cross platform sav
items.content:
Make note, Tenno — Warframe’s Refer a Friend program update is now live! You can take a look at all the need-to-know details below.

Referral Code Changes

You can now create and send referral codes from any platform with a cross platform save enabled account! All you need to do is log into your account on Warframe.com, generate a referral code from the Refer a Friend page, then send that code to the friend you are trying to recruit. After your friend creates their Warframe account, they have seven days to qualify as your recruit. Your friend then inputs your referral code and links your accounts together by either:
  • Entering the referral code in-game by hitting "Communication > Friends > Refer Tenno".
  • Entering the referral code on the Account Management page.

Reward Changes

Both recruiters and recruits can earn Rewards through the new Refer a Friend system.

Recruiter Rewards

There are now four Reward tiers for recruiters to earn depending on how many referrals they send! Recruit 1 Tenno
  • 3x Forma
  • Orokin Catalyst
  • Orokin Reactor
  • Exilus Weapon Adapter
  • Exilus Warframe Adapter
  • Dream Catcher Honoria
Recruit 2 Tenno
  • Two Weapon Slots
Recruit 3 Tenno
  • Warframe Slot
Recruit 4 Tenno
  • Two Archwing Weapon Slots
  • Gravimag
Please note: Once your recruit completes "The Teacher" Quest, you will unlock your recruiter reward! The more Tenno you recruit, the more rewards you get! These Rewards are also retroactive for anyone that qualifies.

Recruit Rewards

After recruits successfully link their accounts, they will instantly gain the following in-game rewards:
  • 7-Day Affinity Booster
  • Classic Color Palette
  • Clan Key

Additional Rewards

Every time one of your recruits makes their first Platinum purchase, 10 percent of the value of Platinum they purchased will be automatically added to your account.

items.title: Warframe's latest update is all about space combat, constellations, and cosmic mythology
items.summary: With its latest major update, Digital Extremes makes space for old mechanics and new ideas in the cosmos.
items.content:
Space Games Warframe's latest update is all about space combat, constellations, and cosmic mythology News By Luke Kemp Last updated 18 June 2026 With its latest major update, Digital Extremes makes space for old mechanics and new ideas in the cosmos.

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

Click for next article (Image credit: Digital Extremes) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Defying recent live service trends in gaming, Warframe has been going strong for 13 years. It's one of the best space games out there, and it shows no signs of slowing down with the Jade Shadows: Constellations update.

The online sci-fi action RPG has evolved both technically and narratively over the years, changing from a game where the story has blossomed from background to the emotion-driven forefront of every major update. In 2024, Jade Shadows started the process of humanising Stalker - the player's long-term nemesis - by making him a father.

Jade Shadows: Constellations serves the dual purpose of continuing this story, and taking the first steps down the road of reinvigorating the game's space-based content.

Watch full video here: At the end of Jade Shadows, players chose one of two names for Stalker's son; Sirius, or Orion. The surprise hook for Constellations is that both came to be… in a way. Bursting onto the scene from parallel timelines, each has come to claim their father for their own. Sirius takes after their mother, while Orion takes after their father. Both are named after notable stellar phenomena: Sirius is the brightest star in the Earth's night sky, while Orion is one of the most prominent constellations visible from Earth (itself named after the legendary hunter in Greek mythology).

Stalker, when he appears during a mission, will relentlessly chase down the player until he or his target dies. What more appropriate name for the son that takes after him, therefore, than the constellation of the Hunter?

Fantasy game 'Crimson Desert' lets you stab your way up to space, showcasing a remarkably detailed cosmos 'I'm still a Trekkie at heart': 'Starseeker: Astroneer Expeditions' takes space exploration games in a friendlier direction (interview) These are the 7 best sci-fi games I saw at Summer Game Fest 2026

"We thought one of the most impactful ways to end a quest would be letting the player name the child," explains Creative Director Rebecca Ford, "and we held that close to our hearts [...] And these names must come from the cosmos, they must come from space. Then we knew we were going to start going divergent paths pretty quickly."

Warframe's missions are spread across the Origin System, which looks very similar to our own solar system . Players jump between planets and moons with real-life equivalents such as Mars , Deimos , Neptune , Mercury , and Earth. The star Sirius and the constellation Orion also exist in the Warframe universe, and factor into the new quest; although exactly how is under wraps at time of writing.

Signup to our newsletter Follow us on Google Constellations is also being used as a springboard for refreshing the game's space battle Railjack content. "I absolutely felt that this update would not work without a Railjack component for the post quest content," explains Ford.

"It did not feel to me like you could do all of this grand space cosmos-like effort without giving players somewhere to use their Railjack. That is a very, very difficult choice in this line of work, where you need to be able to do things very well and very quickly.

"Railjack [is] such a complicated feature, like the code, and the streaming… everything about it is hard, it's making Warframe on hard mode," reveals Ford.

Upcoming space & sci-fi games for 2026 & beyond: Saros, Warhammer 40K: Dawn of War 4, Halo: Campaign Evolved & more 'SOL Shogunate' is a samurai space opera set on the moon, but with an aerospace engineer making sure it's 'getting the science right' Lunar Strike offers a pessimistic, and worryingly realistic look at the future of space travel "In this case we said all right, let's just commit, because we have help from our partners at Sumo, who are co-developing this update, and they love Railjack. So that helped! We just had the stubbornness to say it has to feel this big, it needs this; because it needs to acknowledge that that is how big of an event this quest is."

items.title: Styanax Prime Access
items.summary: Glory calls for Styanax! The master of shield and spear has ascended into Prime Access, offering the steadfastness of an entire phalanx to those bold enough to wield him. Clad in Orokin Gold and outfitted with Exalted manifestations of his
items.content:
Glory calls for Styanax! The master of shield and spear has ascended into Prime Access, offering the steadfastness of an entire phalanx to those bold enough to wield him. Clad in Orokin Gold and outfitted with Exalted manifestations of his iconic Tharros and Axios, this unbreakable champion arrives alongside his signature Prime Weapons and exclusive Prime Accessories. Take advantage of instant access and acquire the Prime items you want by selecting from the Weapons, Prime, Complete and Accessories Packs, depending on your platform and preferences. You can also earn Styanax Prime and his signature Prime Weapons in-game by collecting and cracking Void Relics! https://store.steampowered.com/app/4760260/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Weapons_Pack/ https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/78326/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Prime/ https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/78308/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Complete/ https://store.steampowered.com/app/4760270/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Accessories_Pack/ Styanax Prime Access includes:

Styanax Prime

Shield of the innocent. Spear of justice. Styanax Prime holds the line in defiance of tyranny.

Afentis Prime

The gilded speargun of Styanax Prime shines like a beacon across the battlefield, invigorating allies and stunning enemies.

Athodai Prime

Unleash the song of the righteous warrior with this gilded pistol that goes into Overdrive on headshot kills, maximizing fire rate and ammo efficiency for a short time.

Exclusive Styanax Prime Glyphs

Styanax Prime Accessories:

  • Lanex Prime Syandana
  • Daurus Prime Armor
  • Tharros and Axios Prime Decoration
  • 90-Day Affinity Booster
  • 90-Day Resource Booster
 Note: You'll find the best value in the Styanax Prime Access Complete pack, which includes the Styanax Prime Warframe, his Prime Weapons and Accessories, as well as Platinum!

WHAT ARE PRIMES?

When you require the finest and most powerful armaments, only Primes will do. Exquisitely gilded, every Prime Warframe, Weapon and item exists in its purest form — just as the Orokin intended. If you're new to Warframe, you can learn more about the Prime Access Program by visiting our official FAQs page and watching our community video guide.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Available Now
items.summary: ***Warning: Significant spoilers for the original Jade Shadows Quest follow!*** A rivalry for the ages has officially landed with the arrival of Warframe's latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations! NEW QUEST The consequences continue in
items.content:
***Warning: Significant spoilers for the original Jade Shadows Quest follow!*** A rivalry for the ages has officially landed with the arrival of Warframe's latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations!

NEW QUEST

The consequences continue in a new cinematic sequel Quest that picks up where 2024's Jade Shadows left off. Reassume control of the Stalker as he allies once more with the Tenno to navigate a conflict of celestial proportions. His dueling sons from parallel futures have come to the Origin System in search of their father, and will stop at nothing to claim him for their respective timelines. Only you can end their cycle of conflict and bring harmony to the Origin System!

NEW WARFRAME: SIRIUS & ORION

Dedicated warriors borne of relentless determination. Sirius & Orion comprise the very first dual Warframe, occupying the same slot in your Arsenal while jockeying for position on the battlefield as they rain cosmic destruction upon foes. With their signature Pride and Wrath Scythes you can use brutal heavy attacks to gain Overguard and increased Critical Chance.

Sirius Ability 1: Coronal Ejection

Hurl Sirius' Jade Light infused scythe, dealing Heat Damage while collecting any pickups in its path. Hold to swap to Orion and use Gravitic Slash.

Orion Ability 1: Gravitic Slash

Repel enemies with Orion’s sinister scythe, dealing Slash Damage while reducing shields and armor. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Coronal Ejection.

Sirius Ability 2: Jade Stars

Sirius conjures Jade Light motes that slowly regenerate over time. Attacking enemies launches the motes, dealing Heat Damage. Hold to swap to Orion and use Astral Shell.

Orion Ability 2: Astral Shell

Envelop Orion in an Astral Shell. Upon taking damage the shell becomes a decoy that draws fire until it's destroyed. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Jade Stars.

Sirius Ability 3: Light’s Sanctuary

Sirius creates a well of light that heals and revives allies, while reducing incoming damage. The well slowly grows in size and power. Hold to swap to Orion and use Event Horizon.

Orion Ability 3: Event Horizon

Orion forms a drifting black hole, trapping enemies within its gravity. Hitting the black hole with Gravitic Slash or Coronal Ejection will extend its duration and change its trajectory: Gravitic Slash will direct it away from Orion, and Coronal Ejection will pull it towards Sirius. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Light's Sanctuary.

Sirius & Orion Ability 4: Celestial Clash

Sirius & Orion take to the skies in a cosmic duel. Each attack consumes a Constellation Star to inflict colossal collateral Blast damage. Match the star's color to the Warframes color to gain increased Critical Chance.

NEW PROTOFRAMES: RYOKU AND VENA

Sirius & Orion haven't arrived on their own. Both have trained extensively under the guidance and mentorship of two equally ruthless fighters mired in an intense rivalry of their own. The Ash Protoframe, Ryoku, is a discerning assassin who delivers violence with a disturbing discipline that he has passed along to Sirius. His counterpart, the Garuda Protoframe Vena, indulges her bloodlust with a frenzied brutality befitting her "Wytch" moniker, which she has likewise instilled in Orion.

RETURN TO RAILJACK

Completing the Quest will unlock the Uranus Proxima which includes our most accessible Railjack Missions to date. Here there's no requirement to level up — or even own — a Railjack of your own! Simply enter the Pontis Tower hub, decide whether you'd prefer to fight for Ryoku or Vena, and board their stylized vessels to engage in some of Warframe's most epic space combat yet.

DANTE TYTONIS COLLECTION

Knowledge glides on dark wings with the Dante Tytonis Skin, Noctua Lophos and Gallistrix Warfan Skin. An appropriately avian-themed Collection for the venerable scribe Warframe.

NEW INCARNONS

Five new weapons have been altered by the infernal power of the Void for your destructive pleasure. Wield new Incarnon versions of the Ballistica (Crossbow), Destreza (Rapier), Obex (Sparring), Stug (Pistol), and Vectis (Sniper Rifle), each more indomitable than ever before.

QUALITY OF LIFE IMPROVEMENTS

Nidus Retouch

The avatar of the Infested, Nidus, is more pestilent than ever thanks to an Ability Retouch. Bestowed with significant health boosts and other improvements, this walking plague is both more accessible and enjoyable to wield on the battlefield.

Additional Improvements

As ever, we're improving the overall Warframe experience with several new additions and upgrades, including overhauled lighting in Corpus Ships, adding Damage Over Time (DOT) to enemy Health Bars, enhanced Junction Bosses, Camera offset options, Companion XP adjustments, four new Arcanes and more.

JADE SHADOWS: CONSTELLATIONS PACKS

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735390/Warframe_Constellations_Complete_Collection/ Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations now available alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations!

items.title: Design Mockument: What would a hypothetical Warframe 2 look like?
items.content:
This one has a weird genesis, and I’m going to ask you to roll with me on it because it starts with Guild Wars 3 .

Now that we actually have an announcement, I am relatively sure I’m not exactly alone in expecting GW3 to be one of the next big MMORPGs just like its predecessor. That got me thinking about the Big Five , which arguably at this point should be the Big Four, and which of the games in there could really roll with a sequel to shake things up. Of course, nothing says that GW3 needs to knock its predecessor out of the running, but that also makes me think that the Big Five had some unexpected entries in there alongside even existing as a thing.

So let’s say we’re in a new era, and let’s say GW3 is the vanguard of that new era. That made me start thinking about what theoretical new game could pop up that would be surprising and yet a welcome addition… and herein lies Warframe hypothetically getting a sequel. Let’s say that’s the big announcement at this year’s TennoCon (which I severely doubt). So what would this Warframe 2 even look like in the first place? What would we want it to look like?

Let’s start with something as a foundational concept: Warframe 2 is an MMORPG through and through. Warframe is an MMO that frequently flirts with being an MMORPG, so let’s go all the way and just transfer it. And the best thing is that you don’t even need to worry too much about the setting because in Warframe we already have Eternalism paving the way for us. Which means we’re into a parallel universe, one wherein the war in Tau was won far more decisively, even though the Orokin still fell. The Tenno have awakened once more… in a distant system. Not the Origin system, but somewhere else, guided by Cephalon Margulis.

Fortunately for us, a great deal has changed during the time when the Tenno slept during our long voyage. One of those changes has been a complete revision of how Warframes as a concept work. They have been distilled and improved, turned into a more perfect vessel… because no longer are they based on heroes and identities from the past, but the Tenno themselves.

To start, character creation is now something you start with, creating both your personal Warframe and your Operator from the start. Your Warframe is based broadly on a few basic styles, with numerous morphing changes that you can apply, but frames no longer have distinct identities from one another as they do in the original. A wide array of different skins and helmets from the original are available to define your look right from the beginning.

Abilities, however, are another matter. Each frame has a Core, and each Core is based around a different basic concept. There are six Cores available, and within each Core you can define abilities and modify them based on the five Tennokai schools. In broader strokes, these Cores are based on well-known Warframes from the original game, with a variable cost for abilities, stat modifications, and other alterations based on the abilities you select. So you can mix and match multiple abilities, but if you pick a bunch of really strong abilities and mash them together, you’re going to have a more fragile frame on the whole.

Of course, you can also improve these limits by – yes – polarizing your frame and resetting your abilities back down to a lower level. It’s a combination of several systems into one, in other word, with the net result being you create a custom Warframe tuned the way that you want it.

Weapons are a bit different. Rather than having a primary, secondary, and melee weapon you can equip, you can wield two weapons at a time. For certain weapons, you can wield them simultaneously, like having a glaive in one hand and a pistol in the other, or a pistol or sword in each hand. Other weapons you have to manually swap between, like a rifle and a katana. You can save multiple weapon loadouts and swap between them at weapon lockers, which can sometimes be found mid-mission and are scattered throughout open maps.

Oh, did I not mention that? Yes, this hypothetical game has a lot of open-world map areas. In fact, a lot of the game is actually about traversal. It’s here that the Operator gets to shine; rather than being a specialized ability magnet, Operators are the default in settlements and in the open world, which is much more MMO-styled than even the shared maps existing in the game now. Vehicles are also customized for Operators, allowing you to use different traversal methods to explore the area.

So what about the game’s actual gameplay content? In comes in a few different forms; randomized tilesets with different objectives are available in the style of radio missions from City of Heroes , as well as larger cinematic questlines akin to the current game. More often than not, however, a lot of your missions are more abstract and explorative than that. You need to get certain resources, for example; your objective is to first find where you can get them and how, then farm for them.

Some resources are only found inside of enemy strongholds, and there are certainly several of those. The enemies of the Origin System are not found out here, but the Sentients are, as is a strange form of the Infestation crawling across the initial world. In addition, there are a couple of new factions to contend with, one of which seems to have been pulled from the timeline of the original game, another featuring purely mechanical enemies that nevertheless pose a serious threat.

The main goal of the game overall is to promote a slower and more contemplative vibe than the original while still maintaining the parts of the original that work well. The shooting gameplay of Warframe is exceptionally strong and satisfying, but this provides an opportunity to still get that during missions while also being more willing to slow down. There’s space to expand into some of the other things that the game has done, as well, from railjack exploration to archwings… or to never touch on those again and go into a completely different direction.

Of course, the odds of anything like this happening seem fairly low to me; one of the things that I’m struck by repeatedly is how well Warframe hides its age, and the fact of the matter is that it feels like exactly the experience it wants to be. Trying to make it into more of an MMORPG has actually generally been a thing that didn’t work all that well. Maybe it’s not worth trying, outside of Soulframe , and that’s a different experience to boot.

items.title: Equinox Prime and Wukong Prime Enter Prime Resurgence
items.summary: Equinox Prime From the far edge of fate between day and night, she rises. Equinox Prime harnesses the power of duality to support others — offensively in her Day form and defensively with her Night form. For the heart that seeks equilibrium
items.content: Equinox Prime From the far edge of fate between day and night, she rises. Equinox Prime harnesses the power of duality to support others — offensively in her Day form and defensively with her Night form. For the heart that seeks equilibrium, there’s arguably no better fit than Equinox Prime. Wukong Prime Trickery’s simian avatar possesses strength equal only to his mischievous nature. Survivability is what Wukong Prime is all about, with several of his Abilities allowing him to cheat death or create celestial twins that draw attention from enemies. If keeping foes on their toes is your thing, Wukong Prime should do the trick.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Packs Coming Soon
items.summary: Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations launching alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations! Navigate the conflict between clashing rivals and their mentors as you take up the mantle of the Stalker once more
items.content:
Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations launching alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations! Navigate the conflict between clashing rivals and their mentors as you take up the mantle of the Stalker once more. Jade Shadows: Constellations launches June 17 on all platforms. Now you can take these dueling reflections home by purchasing one of our latest Supporter Packs — either with your local currency from Warframe.com or the in-game Market for Platinum!

Constellations Sirius & Orion Bundle

What might have been becomes reality with Sirius & Orion. Assume the full might of dueling dark futures and follow the stars to constellations of combined power. Featuring:
  • Sirius & Orion Warframe*
  • Pride (Scythe Weapon)
  • Wrath (Scythe Weapon)
  • Constellations Fusion Display
  • 190 Platinum (if purchased with real-world currency)
*Please note that Sirius & Orion are a single Warframe and not two separate Warframes.

Constellations Gemini Collection

Fight an eternal battle for the future with Vena, the blood-loving fanatic, and Ryoku, the cool and calculating killer. Two bitter rivals brought together in this collection of Protoframe Gemini Skins. Each skin can switch between Warframe and Protoframe appearances by activating the corresponding Gemini Emote. Featuring:
  • Ryoku Gemini Skin
  • Ryoku Gemini Emote
  • Vena Gemini Skin
  • Vena Gemini Emote
  • 375 Platinum (if purchased with real-world currency)

Dante Tytonis Collection

Choose the path of wisdom with the Dante Tytonis Collection. His dark wings harbor secret knowledge and arcane power. Featuring:
  • Dante Tytonis Skin
  • Noctua Lophos (Dante's Exalted Tome)
  • Gallistrix Warfan Skin

Constellations Complete Collection

Confront the legacy of a doomed future. Is there room for harmony in the present? Featuring:
  • Constellations Sirius & Orion Bundle
  • Constellations Gemini Collection

Razor's Edge Salon Pack

The future might be doomed, but style doesn’t have to be with this collection featuring Ryoku and Vena’s hairstyles for the Operator and Drifter. Find these bundles in the in-game Market with full regional pricing when Jade Shadows: Constellations launches on June 17!
